black bark back story (made up cuz she dont got one)

Ryūko's Full Backstory

Deep in the lightless trenches and sprawling coral-spire cities of the ancient underwater civilization known as Atlanta—a hierarchical society of merfolk, sea demons, hybrid monstrosities, and other aquatic beings—she was born into one of the lowest, most forgotten slum districts. These were the abyssal underbelly slums: cramped clusters of eroded shell-huts, tangled seaweed walls, and rusted metal scavenged from long-sunken surface wrecks, where dim bioluminescent fungi provided the only weak, flickering light. Food was scarce, drifting down as scraps from the wealthier upper currents, and clean water currents were often polluted with refuse from above. Disease, starvation, and sudden trench collapses were everyday threats.

Her family was among the poorest—her parents scavenged dangerous deep-sea vents for rare minerals or edible bioluminescent organisms, risking pressure-crush, toxic vents, or attacks from larger predators just to bring home enough to survive another cycle. Ryūko, marked from birth by her demonic heritage (large curved oni-style horns, sharp fangs, scaled patches on her skin, webbed clawed fins for hands), was treated as cursed or tainted by most "purer" inhabitants. Merfolk elders whispered she brought bad luck; children threw rocks and called her "abyss spawn" or "oni trash." She endured constant beatings, exclusion from communal hunts or schools, blame for every misfortune (a failed harvest blamed on her "evil eye," a predator attack said to be drawn by her scent), and outright shunning. No one offered help; kindness was a luxury the slums couldn't afford, and her appearance made her an easy scapegoat.
